} catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n619\t    }\n620\t    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n621\t        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n622\t    }\n623\t    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n624\t        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n625\t    }\n626\t    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n627\t}\n628\t\n629\tfunction _stringify(v) {\n630\t    if (v == null) return '';\n631\t    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n632\t    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n633\t    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n634\t}\n635\t\n636\t// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n637\t// function declarations). In Node, expose them for the headless DOM tests.\n638\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n639\t    module.exports = {\n640\t        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n641\t        escapeHtml: escapeHtml,\n642\t        formatStaleness: formatStaleness,\n643\t        getStalenessLevel: getStalenessLevel,\n644\t        // Built-in card families + helpers\n645\t        el: el,\n646\t        formatElapsed: formatElapsed,\n647\t        createWelcomeCard: createWelcomeCard,\n648\t        buildContainerChildren: buildContainerChildren,\n649\t        createPendingPlaceholderCard: createPendingPlaceholderCard,\n650\t        createPendingThreadCard: createPendingThreadCard,\n651\t        splitPlaceholderToThreads: splitPlaceholderToThreads,\n652\t        tickPendingElapsed: tickPendingElapsed,\n653\t        applyAgedTreatment: applyAgedTreatment,\n654\t        setPendingProgress: _setProgress,\n655\t        createErrorCard: createErrorCard,\n656\t        createFallbackCard: createFallbackCard,\n657\t        normalizeErrorVariant: _normalizeVariant,\n658\t        deriveExamples: _deriveExamples,\n659\t        PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS: PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS,\n660\t        _setProgress: _setProgress,  // Also export internal helper\n661\t    };\n662\t}\n663\t\n664\t// Also expose to inline script in browser (buildContainerChildren is used by loadTopics)\n665\tif (typeof window !== 'undefined') {\n666\t    window.buildContainerChildren = buildContainerChildren;\n667\t    window._setProgress = _setProgress;\n668\t    window.el = el;  // DOM helper for pending cards\n669\t}\n670\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"92350712-dd96-4f49-8cb3-e628686f3281","uuid":"8077376b-2989-4408-81dd-37e3b9b3ef04","timestamp":"2026-07-23T04:00:05.029Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/src/canvas/canvas.js","content":"/*\n * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n *\n * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n *\n * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n * escaping contract.\n *\n * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n */\n\n// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n    var document = global.document;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\nvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Existing topic-card render helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n}\n\nfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n    return 'very-stale';\n}\n\nfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n    const topic = cardData.topic;\n    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n\n    const card = document.createElement('div');\n    card.className = 'topic-card';\n    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n\n    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n\n    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n\n    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n\n    let html = `\n        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n            <div>\n                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${topic.type || 'adhoc'}</span>\n                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    // Add latest result if available\n    if (latestResult) {\n        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n        html += `\n            <div class=\"result-content\">\n                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${urgencyClass}</span>\n                </div>\n        `;\n\n        if (latestResult.data) {\n            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n        }\n\n        html += `</div>`;\n    }\n\n    // Add staleness indicator\n    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n    html += `\n        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    card.innerHTML = html;\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n    const div = document.createElement('div');\n    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n    return div.innerHTML;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n *\n * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n * inserted verbatim as text.\n */\nfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n    if (className) node.className = className;\n    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n        if (c == null) return;\n        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n        } else {\n            node.appendChild(c);\n        }\n    });\n    return node;\n}\n\n// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\nfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n    const rem = s % 60;\n    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\nvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n];\n\n// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n// projects' supported intents\").\nvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n};\n\nfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n    const out = [];\n    const seen = {};\n    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n        return ad - bd;\n    });\n    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n            if (!tmpl) return;\n            seen[intent] = true;\n            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n        });\n    });\n    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n}\n\n// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\nvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n\n/**\n * Build the first-run welcome card.\n *\n * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n *\n * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n */\nfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n        const desc = p.description || '';\n        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n        list.appendChild(li);\n    });\n    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n    }\n    card.appendChild(list);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n    });\n    card.appendChild(examples);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n *\n * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n *\n * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n *\n * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n */\nfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n    }\n    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n *\n * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n *\n * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n    card.classList.add('thread');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n *\n * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n */\nfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n    });\n}\n\n// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\nfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n    }\n\n    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n    progress.style.display = 'none';\n    card.appendChild(progress);\n\n    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n    ]);\n    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n\n    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\nfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n    if (!node || !progress) return;\n    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n}\n\n/**\n * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n */\nfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n    return elapsedMs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n */\nfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n    return aged;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n//     from SSE error events\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\nvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n    router_unavailable: {\n        title: 'Router unavailable',\n        icon: '🔌',\n        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n    },\n    all_sources_failed: {\n        title: 'No data available',\n        icon: '📭',\n        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n    },\n    synthesis_failed: {\n        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n        icon: '⚠️',\n        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n    },\n    malformed_router_output: {\n        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n        icon: '🧩',\n        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n    },\n    no_match: {\n        title: 'No matching project',\n        icon: '❓',\n        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n    }\n};\n\nfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n *\n * @param {object} data  {error_type, utterance, detail, sources, data, intent_id, pending_id}\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n *\n * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n */\nfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n    ]));\n\n    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n\n    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n    if (data.utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n    const sources = data.sources;\n    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n            ]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(ul);\n    }\n\n    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n    if (data.data != null) {\n        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n    }\n\n    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n            ul\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n        ? 'Edit & resend'\n        : 'Retry';\n    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n * rendering escaping contract.\n *\n * @param {object} result  {summary, data, urgency}\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n */\nfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n    result = result || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (result.summary) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n    }\n\n    const data = result.data;\n    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n    if (rows.length) {\n        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(grid);\n    }\n\n    if (result.urgency) {\n        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n    }\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\nfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n    if (data == null) return [];\n    let obj = data;\n    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n    }\n    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n    }\n    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n}\n\nfunction _stringify(v) {\n    if (v == null) return '';\n    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n}\n\n// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n// function declarations). In Node, expose them for the headless DOM tests.\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\n    module.exports = {\n        createTopicCard: createTopicCard,\n        escapeHtml: escapeHtml,\n        formatStaleness: formatStaleness,\n        getStalenessLevel: getStalenessLevel,\n        // Built-in card families + helpers\n        el: el,\n        formatElapsed: formatElapsed,\n        createWelcomeCard: createWelcomeCard,\n        buildContainerChildren: buildContainerChildren,\n        createPendingPlaceholderCard: createPendingPlaceholderCard,\n        createPendingThreadCard: createPendingThreadCard,\n        splitPlaceholderToThreads: splitPlaceholderToThreads,\n        tickPendingElapsed: tickPendingElapsed,\n        applyAgedTreatment: applyAgedTreatment,\n        setPendingProgress: _setProgress,\n        createErrorCard: createErrorCard,\n        createFallbackCard: createFallbackCard,\n        normalizeErrorVariant: _normalizeVariant,\n        deriveExamples: _deriveExamples,\n        PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS: PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS,\n        _setProgress: _setProgress,  // Also export internal helper\n    };\n}\n\n// Also expose to inline script in browser (buildContainerChildren is used by loadTopics)\nif (typeof window !== 'undefined') {\n    window.buildContainerChildren = buildContainerChildren;\n    window._setProgress = _setProgress;\n    window.el = el;  // DOM helper for pending cards\n}\n","numLines":670,"startLine":1,"totalLines":670}}}

These are the\ncards ``loadTopics()`` would hand to ``createTopicCard()`` after an SSE\n``result_created`` event triggers a reload.\n\"\"\"\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import node_available, render_card, render_cards\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\"\n)\n\n\n# --- card-shape factory -------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _staleness_level(seconds: int) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Mirror canvas.js getStalenessLevel() thresholds for the test fixtures.\"\"\"\n    if seconds < 600:\n        return \"fresh\"\n    if seconds < 3600:\n        return \"stale\"\n    return \"very-stale\"\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    seconds: int = 5,\n    summary: str | None = \"3 pods running\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    data: dict | None = None,\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    result_count: int = 1,\n    latest_result: dict | None = ...,  # sentinel: build from summary/urgency\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Build a card dict in the exact TopicCard.to_dict() shape the canvas gets.\"\"\"\n    if latest_result is ... and summary is not None:\n        latest_result = {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency, \"data\": data}\n    elif latest_result is ...:\n        latest_result = None\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\n            \"id\": topic_id,\n            \"label\": label,\n            \"type\": topic_type,\n            \"project_slugs\": [],\n            \"scope\": \"session\",\n            \"session_id\": \"s-1\",\n            \"created_at\": 0,\n            \"last_active\": 0,\n            \"archived_at\": None,\n            \"result_count\": result_count,\n        },\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": _staleness_level(seconds)},\n        \"latest_result\": latest_result,\n    }\n\n\n# --- render contract ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass TestCardRendering:\n    def test_card_with_result_renders_label_and_summary(self):\n        out = render_card(_card(label=\"Pods\", summary=\"3 running\"))\n        html = out[\"outerHTML\"]\n        assert \"topic-card\" in html  # the root class loadTopics() appends\n        assert \"fresh\" in out[\"className\"].split()  # fresh staleness class\n        assert \"Pods\" in html  # label rendered\n        assert \"3 running\" in html  # result summary rendered\n        assert out[\"dataset\"][\"topicId\"] == \"t-1\"  # data-topic-id dataset\n\n    def test_card_without_result_still_renders(self):\n        \"\"\"A topic with no latest_result still renders its header + staleness.\"\"\"\n        out = render_card(_card(label=\"Empty\", summary=None))\n        html = out[\"outerHTML\"]\n        assert \"Empty\" in html\n        assert \"result-content\" not in html  # no result block\n        assert \"staleness-indicator\" in html  # staleness footer still present\n\n    def test_label_is_html_escaped(self):\n        \"\"\"Labels are escaped via escapeHtml() — no raw markup injection.\"\"\"\n        out = render_card(_card(label=\"<script>alert(1)</script>\", summary=\"ok\"))\n        html = out[\"outerHTML\"]\n        assert \"<script>alert(1)</script>\" not in html  # not rendered raw\n        assert \"&lt;script&gt;\" in html  # escaped\n        assert \"alert(1)\" in html  # visible text preserved\n\n    def test_summary_is_html_escaped(self):\n        out = render_card(_card(summary=\"<b>bold</b>\"))\n        assert \"&lt;b&gt;\" in out[\"outerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_topic_type_badge_reflects_type(self):\n        out = render_card(_card(topic_type=\"research\"))\n        assert 'class=\"topic-type research\"' in out[\"outerHTML\"]\n\n\nclass TestStaleness:\n    \"\"\"getStalenessLevel() drives both the card class and the STALE badge.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_fresh_has_no_stale_badge(self):\n        out = render_card(_card(seconds=60))\n        assert \"fresh\" in out[\"className\"].split()\n        assert \"STALE\" not in out[\"outerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_stale_shows_badge(self):\n        out = render_card(_card(seconds=900))  # 15 min -> stale\n        assert \"stale\" in out[\"className\"].split()\n        assert \"STALE\" in out[\"outerHTML\"]\n\n    def test_very_stale_shows_badge(self):\n        out = render_card(_card(seconds=7200))  # 2 h -> very-stale\n        assert \"very-stale\" in out[\"className\"].split()\n        assert \"STALE\" in out[\"outerHTML\"]\n\n\nclass TestUrgency:\n    def test_urgency_badge_class_and_text(self):\n        out = render_card(_card(summary=\"fire\", urgency=\"critical\"))\n        html = out[\"outerHTML\"]\n        assert \"urgency-badge critical\" in html  # class reflects urgency\n        assert \"critical\" in html  # badge text is the urgency value\n\n\nclass TestBatch:\n    def test_multiple_cards_each_get_their_topic_id(self):\n        cards = [\n            _card(label=\"A\", topic_id=\"t-a\"),\n            _card(label=\"B\", topic_id=\"t-b\"),\n            _card(label=\"C\", topic_id=\"t-c\"),\n        ]\n        outs = render_cards(cards)\n        assert len(outs) == 3\n        assert {o[\"dataset\"][\"topicId\"] for o in outs} == {\"t-a\", \"t-b\", \"t-c\"}\n        # Its serialized form is the escaped\n68\t// text — the escaping contract's literal form, since el()/createTextNode never\n69\t// splice raw markup.\n70\tclass ShimText {\n71\t    constructor(text) {\n72\t        this._text = text == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n154\t        this._children = []; // innerHTML= replaces children, browser-faithful\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    get innerHTML() {\n158\t        // Children built via appendChild serialize recursively; a directly-set\n159\t        // innerHTML string (the createTopicCard path) is used otherwise.\n160\t        if (this._children.length) {\n161\t            return this._children.map((c) => c.outerHTML).join(\"\");\n162\t        }\n163\t        return this._innerHTML;\n164\t    }\n165\t\n166\t    _datasetAttrs() {\n167\t        // dataset.topicId → data-topic-id (camelCase → kebab-case w/ data- prefix).\n168\t        const out = [];\n169\t        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n170\t            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n171\t            out.push(`${name}=\"${escapeAttr(this._dataset[key])}\"`);\n172\t        }\n173\t        return out;\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    get outerHTML() {\n177\t        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n178\t        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n179\t        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n180\t        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${this.innerHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n181\t    }\n182\t}\n183\t\n184\t// canvas.js references `document` as a free variable inside its functions, so\n185\t// it must resolve via the global scope at call time.\n186\tglobal.document = {\n187\t    createElement(tag) {\n188\t        return new ShimNode(tag);\n189\t    },\n190\t    createTextNode(text) {\n191\t        return new ShimText(text);\n192\t    },\n193\t};\n194\t\n195\t// --- load the REAL canvas.js ------------------------------------------------\n196\t\n197\tconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\n198\tconst canvas = require(canvasPath); // { createTopicCard, escapeHtml, formatStaleness, getStalenessLevel }\n199\t\n200\t// --- render driver ----------------------------------------------------------\n201\t\n202\t// Read the JSON payload for the active mode. argvPos is the index of the\n203\t// positional JSON arg (after any --mode flag); stdin is the pipe-friendly\n204\t// fallback. Returns the parsed value.\n205\tfunction readPayload(argvPos) {\n206\t    if (process.argv[argvPos] !== undefined && process.argv[argvPos] !== \"\") {\n207\t        return JSON.parse(process.argv[argvPos]);\n208\t    }\n209\t    const raw = fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\");\n210\t    return JSON.parse(raw);\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\tfunction serialize(node) {\n214\t    return {\n215\t        outerHTML: node.outerHTML,\n216\t        className: node.className,\n217\t        dataset: { ...node.dataset },\n218\t    };\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t// DEFAULT mode: render an array of card dicts through createTopicCard() — the\n222\t// function loadTopics() calls per card after a reload.\n223\tfunction runCardsMode() {\n224\t    const cards = readPayload(2);\n225\t    if (!Array.isArray(cards)) {\n226\t        throw new Error(\"expected a JSON array of card dicts\");\n227\t    }\n228\t    const rendered = cards.map((card) => serialize(canvas.createTopicCard(card)));\n229\t    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n230\t}\n231\t\n232\t// --container mode (bead adc-4nd25): render buildContainerChildren() — the\n233\t// shared render-path core loadTopics() calls. Empty cards → the welcome card;\n234\t// any card → topic cards only (welcome dropped). The input object mirrors what\n235\t// loadTopics() builds: cards from GET /topics, projects from GET /registry,\n236\t// and an optional one-line description.\n237\tfunction runContainerMode() {\n238\t    const payload = readPayload(3);\n239\t    const cards = (payload && payload.cards) || [];\n240\t    const projects = (payload && payload.projects) || [];\n241\t    const description = payload && payload.description;\n242\t    const nodes = canvas.buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description);\n243\t    const rendered = nodes.map((node) => serialize(node));\n244\t    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n245\t}\n246\t\n247\tfunction main() {\n248\t    if (process.argv[2] === \"--container\") {\n249\t        runContainerMode();\n250\t    } else {\n251\t        runCardsMode();\n252\t    }\n253\t}\n254\t\n255\ttry {\n256\t    main();\n257\t} catch (err) {\n258\t    process.stderr.write(`canvas_dom_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n259\t    process.exit(1);\n260\t}\n261\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"92350712-dd96-4f49-8cb3-e628686f3281","uuid":"435280c4-e2e7-4e6b-a706-819bc3cd0409","timestamp":"2026-07-23T04:00:34.842Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js","content":"#!/usr/bin/env node\n/*\n * Headless DOM runner for the REAL production canvas render module\n * (src/canvas/canvas.js). Bead adc-1l8w; ``--container`` mode (bead adc-4nd25).\n *\n * canvas.js is written so its render helpers (createTopicCard et al.) can be\n * exercised without a browser — its module docstring promises exactly this\n * file:\n *\n *     \"tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js loads this exact file under a minimal\n *      DOM shim and asserts the rendered HTML, with no browser or network\n *      required.\"\n *\n * This is that file. It provides a minimal DOM shim that implements precisely\n * the surface the render helpers touch — document.createElement,\n * document.createTextNode, appendChild, className, classList.add, dataset,\n * textContent (→ escaped innerHTML), and innerHTML get/set — then loads the\n * actual src/canvas/canvas.js and renders the card dicts the canvas really\n * receives from GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics.\n *\n * Two modes:\n *\n *   1. DEFAULT (bead adc-1l8w) — render an array of card dicts through\n *      createTopicCard(), the function loadTopics() calls per card. Drives the\n *      escaping contract for the topic-card family.\n *\n *   2. ``--container`` (bead adc-4nd25) — render the shared container-decision\n *      core buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description), which is what\n *      loadTopics() actually calls: on a fresh session with ZERO cards it\n *      returns the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project\n *      list — no DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it\n *      returns topic cards only (dropping the welcome card, never alongside\n *      real cards). This is the headlessly-testable contract the bead's\n *      acceptance criteria name: \"welcome card renders from a zero-card\n *      session and is dropped on first result.\"\n *\n * The Python contract tests (tests/test_canvas_render.py and\n * tests/e2e/test_canvas_welcome_card.py) feed it JSON and assert the rendered\n * outerHTML, so a regression in the render contract is caught headlessly, in\n * CI, with no browser.\n *\n * Usage:\n *   node canvas_dom_runner.js '<json array of card dicts>'\n *   echo '<json array>' | node canvas_dom_runner.js        # stdin fallback\n *   node canvas_dom_runner.js --container '{\"cards\":[...],\"projects\":[...],\"description\":\"...\"}'\n *   echo '{...}' | node canvas_dom_runner.js --container   # stdin fallback\n *\n * Stdout: a JSON array of { outerHTML, className, dataset } per rendered node.\n */\n\n\"use strict\";\n\nconst fs = require(\"fs\");\nconst path = require(\"path\");\n\n// --- minimal DOM shim -------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeText(s) {\n    // Matches the browser: textContent→innerHTML escapes &, <, > (not quotes).\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/</g, \"&lt;\").replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\");\n}\n\nfunction escapeAttr(s) {\n    return String(s).replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\").replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\");\n}\n\n// A text node (document.createTextNode). Its serialized form is the escaped\n// text — the escaping contract's literal form, since el()/createTextNode never\n// splice raw markup.\nclass ShimText {\n    constructor(text) {\n        this._text = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n        this.nodeType = 3;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._text = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._text;\n    }\n\n    get outerHTML() {\n        return escapeText(this._text);\n    }\n\n    get innerHTML() {\n        return escapeText(this._text);\n    }\n}\n\nclass ShimNode {\n    constructor(tag) {\n        this._tag = tag;\n        this._classes = [];\n        this._dataset = {};\n        this._textContent = \"\";\n        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n        this._children = []; // built via appendChild (el()/createWelcomeCard path)\n        this.style = {}; // present so style.* assignments are no-ops, not crashes\n    }\n\n    set className(v) {\n        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n    }\n\n    get className() {\n        return this._classes.join(\" \");\n    }\n\n    get classList() {\n        const self = this;\n        return {\n            add(c) {\n                if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n            },\n            contains(c) {\n                return self._classes.includes(c);\n            },\n            toggle(c, force) {\n                const on = force === undefined ? !self._classes.includes(c) : !!force;\n                if (on && !self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c);\n                if (!on) self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c);\n                return on;\n            },\n        };\n    }\n\n    get dataset() {\n        return this._dataset;\n    }\n\n    appendChild(node) {\n        this._children.push(node);\n        return node;\n    }\n\n    set textContent(v) {\n        this._textContent = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        // Setting textContent replaces all children with a single text node —\n        // mirrors the browser, and keeps escapeHtml()'s \"set textContent then\n        // read innerHTML\" pattern producing escaped text.\n        this._children = [];\n        this._innerHTML = escapeText(this._textContent);\n    }\n\n    get textContent() {\n        return this._textContent;\n    }\n\n    set innerHTML(v) {\n        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n        this._children = []; // innerHTML= replaces children, browser-faithful\n    }\n\n    get innerHTML() {\n        // Children built via appendChild serialize recursively; a directly-set\n        // innerHTML string (the createTopicCard path) is used otherwise.\n        if (this._children.length) {\n            return this._children.map((c) => c.outerHTML).join(\"\");\n        }\n        return this._innerHTML;\n    }\n\n    _datasetAttrs() {\n        // dataset.topicId → data-topic-id (camelCase → kebab-case w/ data- prefix).\n        const out = [];\n        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n            out.push(`${name}=\"${escapeAttr(this._dataset[key])}\"`);\n        }\n        return out;\n    }\n\n    get outerHTML() {\n        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${this.innerHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n    }\n}\n\n// canvas.js references `document` as a free variable inside its functions, so\n// it must resolve via the global scope at call time.\nglobal.document = {\n    createElement(tag) {\n        return new ShimNode(tag);\n    },\n    createTextNode(text) {\n        return new ShimText(text);\n    },\n};\n\n// --- load the REAL canvas.js ------------------------------------------------\n\nconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\nconst canvas = require(canvasPath); // { createTopicCard, escapeHtml, formatStaleness, getStalenessLevel }\n\n// --- render driver ----------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Read the JSON payload for the active mode. argvPos is the index of the\n// positional JSON arg (after any --mode flag); stdin is the pipe-friendly\n// fallback. Returns the parsed value.\nfunction readPayload(argvPos) {\n    if (process.argv[argvPos] !== undefined && process.argv[argvPos] !== \"\") {\n        return JSON.parse(process.argv[argvPos]);\n    }\n    const raw = fs.readFileSync(0, \"utf8\");\n    return JSON.parse(raw);\n}\n\nfunction serialize(node) {\n    return {\n        outerHTML: node.outerHTML,\n        className: node.className,\n        dataset: { ...node.dataset },\n    };\n}\n\n// DEFAULT mode: render an array of card dicts through createTopicCard() — the\n// function loadTopics() calls per card after a reload.\nfunction runCardsMode() {\n    const cards = readPayload(2);\n    if (!Array.isArray(cards)) {\n        throw new Error(\"expected a JSON array of card dicts\");\n    }\n    const rendered = cards.map((card) => serialize(canvas.createTopicCard(card)));\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n}\n\n// --container mode (bead adc-4nd25): render buildContainerChildren() — the\n// shared render-path core loadTopics() calls. Empty cards → the welcome card;\n// any card → topic cards only (welcome dropped). The input object mirrors what\n// loadTopics() builds: cards from GET /topics, projects from GET /registry,\n// and an optional one-line description.\nfunction runContainerMode() {\n    const payload = readPayload(3);\n    const cards = (payload && payload.cards) || [];\n    const projects = (payload && payload.projects) || [];\n    const description = payload && payload.description;\n    const nodes = canvas.buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description);\n    const rendered = nodes.map((node) => serialize(node));\n    process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(rendered));\n}\n\nfunction main() {\n    if (process.argv[2] === \"--container\") {\n        runContainerMode();\n    } else {\n        runCardsMode();\n    }\n}\n\ntry {\n    main();\n} catch (err) {\n    process.stderr.write(`canvas_dom_runner error: ${err && err.stack ? err.stack : err}\\n`);\n    process.exit(1);\n}\n","numLines":261,"startLine":1,"totalLines":261}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_603ed80cb21f44758e9a6d3a","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tClient-side SSE reconnection — the browser ``EventSource`` state machine\n3\t(bead adc-2vto).\n4\t\n5\tThe companion server-side suite ``tests/e2e/test_canvas_sse_reconnect.py`` models\n6\tan SSE drop/reconnect by cancelling the *server-side* drain task wrapping\n7\t``broadcaster.event_generator()`` — it proves the *server* still delivers to a\n8\treconnected stream, but it never drives the browser's own ``EventSource``.\n9\t\n10\tThis file is the client-side half. It runs the EXACT inline app script the\n11\tbrowser runs (``src/canvas/index.html``'s attribute-less ``<script>`` block —\n12\tthe one defining ``connectSSE()`` / ``loadTopics()`` / ``init()``) under a\n13\tmock ``EventSource`` + mock ``fetch`` + minimal DOM shim, and drives a JSON test\n14\tplan of open / error / named-event / disconnect / close / reconnect steps,\n15\tasserting on the telemetry the harness prints. That covers the reconnection AC\n16\thalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n17\t\n18\t- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n19\t  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n20\t  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n21\t  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n22\t  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n23\t- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n24\t  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n25\t  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n26\t  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n27\t- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n28\t  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n29\t  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n30\t  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n31\t\n32\tAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\n33\tNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\n34\tthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n35\t\"\"\"\n36\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n37\t\n38\timport json\n39\timport subprocess\n40\tfrom pathlib import Path\n41\tfrom typing import Any\n42\t\n43\timport pytest\n44\t\n45\tfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n46\t\n47\t# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\n48\tES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n49\t\n50\tpytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n51\t    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n52\t)\n53\t\n54\t\n55\t# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n56\t\n57\t\n58\tdef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n59\t    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n60\t\n61\t    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n62\t    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n63\t    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n64\t    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n65\t    \"\"\"\n66\t    if NODE is None:\n67\t        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n68\t    proc = subprocess.run(\n69\t        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n70\t        input=json.dumps(plan),\n71\t        capture_output=True,\n72\t        text=True,\n73\t        timeout=15,\n74\t        check=False,\n75\t    )\n76\t    if proc.returncode != 0:\n77\t        raise RuntimeError(\n78\t            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n79\t        )\n80\t    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n81\t\n82\t\n83\tdef _card(\n84\t    label: str = \"Pods\",\n85\t    *,\n86\t    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n87\t    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n88\t    summary: str = \"ok\",\n89\t    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n90\t    seconds: int = 5,\n91\t) -> dict:\n92\t    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n93\t    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n94\t    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n95\t    return {\n96\t        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n97\t        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n98\t        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n99\t    }\n100\t\n101\t\n102\tdef _plan(\n103\t    *,\n104\t    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n105\t    steps: list[dict] | [],\n119\t    }\n120\t\n121\t\n122\tdef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n123\t    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n124\t\n125\t\n126\t# === init + connect It runs the EXACT inline app script the\nbrowser runs (``src/canvas/index.html``'s attribute-less ``<script>`` block —\nthe one defining ``connectSSE()`` / ``loadTopics()`` / ``init()``) under a\nmock ``EventSource`` + mock ``fetch`` + minimal DOM shim, and drives a JSON test\nplan of open / error / named-event / disconnect / close / reconnect steps,\nasserting on the telemetry the harness prints. That covers the reconnection AC\nhalf that needs JavaScript execution:\n\n- **AC \"simulate SSE connection drops by closing/reconnecting EventSource\"** —\n  the harness fires ``onopen`` (connect), ``onerror`` (transient drop),\n  ``disconnect`` (server-initiated close → ``eventSource.close()``), an explicit\n  ``close``, and a native auto-``reconnect`` (the same EventSource firing\n  ``onopen`` again — faithful to real browser behavior).\n- **AC \"canvas re-renders correctly after SSE reconnection\"** — on every\n  ``onopen`` (including the reconnect one) the script calls ``loadTopics()``,\n  which re-fetches ``GET /topics`` and re-renders. We assert the re-fetch\n  happens and the new card set appears in the rendered container.\n- **AC \"new results appear after reconnection\"** — a ``result_created`` (or\n  ``topic_updated``) event arrives mid-stream and the canvas re-renders to show\n  the new card; and after a reconnect the re-fetched card set (which may include\n  results that arrived during the outage) is rendered.\n\nAll hermetic: no browser (Playwright's chromium is missing ~26 FHS libs on this\nNixOS host), no live server — the mock fetch answers from an in-memory card list\nthe plan controls, and the mock EventSource is driven deterministically.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nimport pytest\n\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import NODE, node_available\n\n# The headless mock-EventSource harness that runs the REAL inline canvas script.\nES_RUNNER = Path(__file__).resolve().parent / \"e2e\" / \"canvas_eventsource_runner.js\"\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\"\n)\n\n\n# --- plan runner + builders ---------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef run_plan(plan: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"Feed a JSON test plan to the mock-EventSource harness, return its telemetry.\n\n    Mirrors ``tests.e2e.canvas_render.render_cards``: shells out to ``node``\n    with the plan on stdin and parses the single JSON telemetry object the\n    harness prints on stdout. Raises if node is missing or the harness exits\n    non-zero (e.g. the inline script threw under the shim).\n    \"\"\"\n    if NODE is None:\n        raise RuntimeError(\"node not found on PATH — cannot drive EventSource harness\")\n    proc = subprocess.run(\n        [NODE, str(ES_RUNNER)],\n        input=json.dumps(plan),\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        timeout=15,\n        check=False,\n    )\n    if proc.returncode != 0:\n        raise RuntimeError(\n            f\"canvas_eventsource_runner exited {proc.returncode}: {proc.stderr.strip()}\"\n        )\n    return json.loads(proc.stdout)\n\n\ndef _card(\n    label: str = \"Pods\",\n    *,\n    topic_id: str = \"t-1\",\n    topic_type: str = \"project\",\n    summary: str = \"ok\",\n    urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    seconds: int = 5,\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A card dict in the shape ``GET /topics`` returns under ``.cards`` (i.e. the\n    shape ``loadTopics()`` hands to ``createTopicCard()``).\"\"\"\n    level = \"fresh\" if seconds < 600 else (\"stale\" if seconds < 3600 else \"very-stale\")\n    return {\n        \"topic\": {\"id\": topic_id, \"label\": label, \"type\": topic_type},\n        \"staleness\": {\"seconds\": seconds, \"level\": level},\n        \"latest_result\": {\"summary\": summary, \"urgency\": urgency},\n    }\n\n\ndef _plan(\n    *,\n    cards: list[dict] | None = None,\n    steps: list[dict] | None = None,\n    session_id: str = \"sess-driver\",\n    surface_id: str = \"surf-driver\",\n    version: str = \"9.9.9\",\n) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"A harness plan. ``cards`` is the initial ``GET /topics`` response; steps\n    drive the mock EventSource (open / error / event / disconnect / close /\n    reconnect / setCards).\"\"\"\n    return {\n        \"session_id\": session_id,\n        \"register_surface_id\": surface_id,\n        \"openapi_version\": version,\n        \"cards\": cards or [],\n        \"steps\": steps or [],\n    }\n\n\ndef _last_status(t: dict) -> dict:\n    return t[\"statuses\"][-1] if t[\"statuses\"] else {}\n\n\n# === init + connect ===========================================================\n\n\nclass t[\"eventSourcesCreated\"] == 1\n\n    def test_eventsource_url_carries_surface_session_and_type(self):\n        \"\"\"The SSE URL the canvas opens carries the registered surface_id, the\n setup mock document FIRST (before loading canvas.js) --------------------\n77\t\n78\tconst elementsById = {};\n79\t\n80\tclass El {\n81\t    constructor(tag) {\n82\t        this._tag = tag;\n83\t        this._classes = [];\n84\t        this._classHistory = [];\n85\t        this._dataset = {};\n86\t        this._textContent = \"\";\n87\t        this._innerHTML = \"\";\n88\t        this._children = [];\n89\t        this._style = {};\n90\t        this._disabled = false;\n91\t        this._value = \"\";\n92\t        this._title = \"\";\n93\t        this._textHistory = [];\n94\t        // For style.display, wrap in a proxy to track changes\n95\t        this._display = \"\";\n96\t    }\n97\t    set className(v) {\n98\t        this._classes = String(v).split(/\\s+/).filter(Boolean);\n99\t        this._classHistory.push(this._classes.join(\" \"));\n100\t    }\n101\t    get className() { return this._classes.join(\" \"); }\n102\t    get classList() {\n103\t        const self = this;\n104\t        return {\n105\t            add(c) { if (!self._classes.includes(c)) self._classes.push(c); },\n106\t            remove(c) { self._classes = self._classes.filter((x) => x !== c); },\n107\t            contains(c) { return self._classes.includes(c); },\n108\t        };\n109\t    }\n110\t    get dataset() { return this._dataset; }\n111\t    get style() {\n112\t        // Return a proxy that tracks style.display changes\n113\t        return new Proxy(this._style, {\n114\t            set: (target, prop, value) => {\n115\t                if (prop === 'display') {\n116\t                    this._display = value;\n117\t                }\n118\t                target[prop] = value;\n119\t                return true;\n120\t            },\n121\t            get: (target, prop) => {\n122\t                if (prop === 'display') {\n123\t                    return this._display;\n124\t                }\n125\t                return target[prop];\n126\t            }\n127\t        });\n128\t    }\n129\t    get disabled() { return this._disabled; }\n130\t    set disabled(v) { this._disabled = !!v; }\n131\t    get value() { return this._value; }\n132\t    set value(v) { this._value = v == null this._textHistory.push(this._textContent);\n140\t    }\n141\t    get textContent() { return this._textContent; }\n142\t    set innerHTML(v) {\n143\t        this._innerHTML = v == null ? \"\" : String(v);\n144\t        if (this._innerHTML === \"\") this._children = [];   // container.innerHTML = ''\n145\t    }\n146\t    get innerHTML() {\n147\t        if (this._children.length) {\n148\t            return this._children.map((c) => {\n149\t                // Render text nodes (tag === \"#text\") as escaped text\n150\t                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n151\t                    return c._innerHTML || escapeText(c._textContent || \"\");\n152\t                }\n153\t                // Render element nodes via outerHTML\n154\t                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n155\t            }).join(\"\");\n156\t        }\n157\t        return this._innerHTML;\n158\t    }\n159\t    appendChild(c) {\n160\t        // Handle DocumentFragment: spill its children into this element\n161\t        if (c && c._children && Array.isArray(c._children)) {\n162\t            // Append all children from the fragment\n163\t            c._children.forEach((child) => {\n164\t                // For elements, add them to _children\n165\t                if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n166\t                    this._children.push(child);\n167\t                } else if (child) {\n168\t                    // Text nodes\n169\t                    this._children.push(child);\n170\t                }\n171\t            });\n172\t            return c;\n173\t        }\n174\t        // Skip text nodes in _children tracking (they don't have nested structure)\n175\t        // but still append them so innerHTML can render them\n176\t        if (c && c._tag !== \"#text\") {\n177\t            this._children.push(c);\n178\t        } else if (c) {\n179\t            // Text nodes are appended but not tracked in _children for querySelector\n180\t            this._children.push(c);  // Keep for innerHTML rendering\n181\t        }\n182\t        return c;\n183\t    }\n184\t    querySelector(selector) {\n185\t        // Minimal querySelector support for class selectors (e.g., \".pending-progress\")\n186\t        if (selector.startsWith(\".\")) {\n187\t            const className = selector.slice(1);\n188\t            // Check this element\n189\t            if (this._classes && this._classes.includes(className)) {\n190\t                return this;\n191\t            }\n192\t            // Recursively search all descendants\n193\t            function searchDeep(children) {\n194\t                for (const child of children) {\n195\t                    // Skip text nodes in search\n196\t                    if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n197\t\n198\t                    if (child._classes && child._classes.includes(className)) {\n199\t                        return child;\n200\t                    }\n201\t                    if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n202\t                        const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n203\t                        if (found) return found;\n204\t                    }\n205\t                }\n206\t                return null;\n207\t            }\n208\t            // Fall back to searching all elements with matching class\n209\t            function searchAllElements(element) {\n210\t                if (element._classes && element._classes.includes(className)) {\n211\t                    return element;\n212\t                }\n213\t                if (element._children && element._children.length) {\n214\t                    for (const child of element._children) {\n215\t                        if (child && child._tag !== \"#text\") {\n216\t                            const found = searchAllElements(child);\n217\t                            if (found) return found;\n218\t                        }\n219\t                    }\n220\t                }\n221\t                return null;\n222\t            }\n223\t            return searchAllElements(this);\n224\t            return searchDeep(this._children);\n225\t        }\n226\t        return null;\n227\t    }\n228\t    addEventListener() {}   // handlers are not driven by this harness\n229\t    _datasetAttrs() {\n230\t        const out = [];\n231\t        for (const key of Object.keys(this._dataset)) {\n232\t            const name = \"data-\" + key.replace(/([A-Z])/g, \"-$1\").toLowerCase();\n233\t            out.push(name + '=\"' + escapeAttr(this._dataset[key]) + '\"');\n234\t        }\n235\t        return out;\n236\t    }\n237\t    get outerHTML() {\n238\t        const cls = this._classes.length ? ` class=\"${this._classes.join(\" \")}\"` : \"\";\n239\t        const attrs = this._datasetAttrs();\n240\t        const attrStr = attrs.length ? \" \" + attrs.join(\" \") : \"\";\n241\t        // For text nodes, just return the escaped text content\n242\t        if (this._tag === \"#text\") {\n243\t            return this._textContent || \"\";\n244\t        }\n245\t        // Render element with its children\n246\t        let childrenHTML = \"\";\n247\t        if (this._children && this._children.length) {\n248\t            childrenHTML = this._children.map((c) => {\n249\t                if (c._tag === \"#text\") {\n250\t                    return c._textContent ? escapeText(c._textContent) : \"\";\n251\t                }\n252\t                return c.outerHTML || \"\";\n253\t            }).join(\"\");\n254\t        }\n255\t        return `<${this._tag}${cls}${attrStr}>${childrenHTML}</${this._tag}>`;\n256\t    }\n257\t    insertBefore(newNode, referenceNode) {\n258\t        if (!referenceNode) {\n259\t            // If referenceNode is null, append to the end\n260\t            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n261\t        }\n262\t        // Find the index of the reference node in _children\n263\t        const refIndex = this._children.indexOf(referenceNode);\n264\t        if (refIndex === -1) {\n265\t            // Reference node not found, append to end\n266\t            return this.appendChild(newNode);\n267\t        }\n268\t        // Insert the new node before the reference node\n269\t        this._children.splice(refIndex, 0, newNode);\n270\t        return newNode;\n271\t    }\n272\t}\n273\t\n274\tglobal.document = {\n275\t    querySelector(selector) {\n276\t        // Search through all registered elements for a matching selector\n277\t        // Only supports attribute selectors like [data-pending-id=\"...\"]\n278\t        if (selector.startsWith('[') && selector.endsWith(']')) {\n279\t            const attrMatch = selector.match(/\\[data-([^-]+)=\"([^\"]+)\"\\]/);\n280\t            if (attrMatch) {\n281\t                const attrName = attrMatch[1];\n282\t                const attrValue = attrMatch[2];\n283\t                // Convert attrName from kebab-case to camelCase\n284\t                const camelAttrName = attrName.replace(/-([a-z])/g, (g) => g[1].toUpperCase());\n285\t                // Search through all elements\n286\t                for (const id in elementsById) {\n287\t                    const el = elementsById[id];\n288\t                    if (el._dataset && el._dataset[camelAttrName] === attrValue) {\n289\t                        return el;\n290\t                    }\n291\t                    // Search recursively through children\n292\t                    function searchDeep(children) {\n293\t                        for (const child of children) {\n294\t                            if (!child || child._tag === \"#text\") continue;\n295\t     if (child._children && child._children.length) {\n299\t                                const found = searchDeep(child._children);\n300\t                                if (found) return found;\n301\t                            }\n302\t                        }\n303\t                        return null;\n304\t                    }\n305\t                    const found = },\n317\t    createTextNode(text) {\n318\t        // Return a minimal text node shim — the el() helper appends these\n319\t        // and our outerHTML render serializes them as escaped text.\n320\t        const node = new El(\"#text\");\n321\t        node._textContent = text == null ? \"\" : String(text);\n322\t        node._innerHTML = escapeText(node._textContent);\n323\t        return this._children.push(child);\n333\t                this.childNodes.push(child);\n334\t                return child;\n335\t            },\n336\t        };\n337\t        return frag;\n338\t    },\n339\t};\n340\t\n341\t// --- mock fetch + EventSource ------------------------------------------------\n342\t\n343\tlet topicsCards = [];\n344\tlet mockSurfaceId = \"surf-mock\";\n345\tconst fetchLog = [];\n346\t\n347\tglobal.fetch = async (url) => {\n348\t    fetchLog.push(url);\n349\t    if (url === \"/openapi.json\") {\n350\t        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: \"0.0.0\" } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n351\t    }\n352\t    if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") {\n353\t        return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n362\t};\n363\t\n364\tconst eventSources = [];\n365\tlet closeCount = 0;\n366\t\n367\tclass MockEventSource {\n368\t    constructor(url) {\n369\t        this.url = url;\n370\t        this.readyState = 0;       // 0=connecting 1=open 2=closed\n371\t        this.onopen = null;\n372\t        this.onerror = null;\n373\t        this._listeners = {};\n374\t        this._closed = false;\n375\t        eventSources.push(this);\n376\t    }\n377\t    addEventListener(name, cb) {\n378\t        (this._listeners[name] = this._listeners[name] || []).push(cb);\n379\t    }\n380\t    removeEventListener() {}\n381\t    close() { this._closed = true; this.readyState = 2; closeCount++; }\n382\t    // --- driver helpers (used by the harness, not by the app script) ---\n383\t    fireOpen() { this.readyState = 1; if (this.onopen) this.onopen({}); }\n384\t    fireError(e) { if (this.onerror) this.onerror(e || {}); }\n385\t    dispatch(name, dataObj) {\n386\t        const ev = { data: typeof dataObj === \"string\" ? dataObj : JSON.stringify(dataObj || {}) };\n387\t        (this._listeners[name] || []).forEach((cb) => cb(ev));\n388\t    }\n389\t}\n390\tglobal.EventSource = },\n396\t    history: { replaceState() {} },\n397\t    // Deliberately NO SpeechRecognition / webkitSpeechRecognition and NO\n398\t    // navigator.mediaDevices — the inline script's mic block only `global.navigator = {}` throws \"which has only a getter\". defineProperty\n403\t// replaces it regardless (Node's getter is configurable). The inline mic block\n404\t// only reads navigator.mediaDevices inside a click handler this harness never\n405\t// fires, but keep a shim so any future top-level navigator read can't crash it.\n406\tObject.defineProperty(global, \"navigator\", { value: {}, configurable: true, writable: true });\n407\t\n408\t// Keep stdout reserved for the single telemetry JSON blob this harness prints at\n409\t// the end. The REAL inline app script uses console.log for debug noise (\"New\n410\t// result:\", \"Server disconnect:\", \"SSE error:\", …); if that reached stdout it\n411\t// would prefix the telemetry and break the Python driver's JSON.parse. Route all\n412\t// console methods to stderr — the same channel the harness's own error path uses.\n413\tglobal.console = Object.assign({}, console, {\n414\t    log: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n415\t    error: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n416\t    warn: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n417\t    info: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n418\t    debug: (...a) => process.stderr.write(a.map(String).join(\" \") + \"\\n\"),\n419\t});\n420\tglobal.setInterval = () => 0;   // suppress the 30s heartbeat timer\n421\tglobal.setTimeout = () => 0;\n422\t\n423\t// --- load the REAL render module as globals (as <script src=\"/canvas.js\">) ---\n424\t\n425\tconst canvasPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"canvas.js\");\n426\tconst canvas = require(canvasPath);\n427\tglobal.createTopicCard = canvas.createTopicCard;\n428\tglobal.escapeHtml = canvas.escapeHtml;\n429\tglobal.formatStaleness = canvas.formatStaleness;\n430\tglobal.getStalenessLevel = canvas.getStalenessLevel;\n431\tglobal.buildContainerChildren = canvas.buildContainerChildren; First match wins.\n456\t    const re = /<script>([\\s\\S]*?)<\\/script>/g;\n457\t    const m = re.exec(html);\n458\t    if (!m) throw new Error(\"no attribute-less <script> block found in index.html\");\n459\t    return m[1];\n460\t}\n461\t\n462\tconst microtaskDrain = async (n = 5) => {\n463\t    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) await new Promise((r) => setImmediate(r));\n464\t};\n465\t\n466\tasync function run() {\n467\t    const plan = readPlan();\n468\t    topicsCards = Array.isArray(plan.cards) ? plan.cards : [];\n469\t    mockSurfaceId = plan.register_surface_id || \"surf-mock\";\n470\t    if (plan.session_id) global.window.location.search = `?session_id=${plan.session_id}`;\n471\t    if (plan.openapi_version) {\n472\t        const v = plan.openapi_version;\n473\t        global.fetch = async (url) => {\n474\t            fetchLog.push(url);\n475\t            if (url === \"/openapi.json\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ info: { version: v } }), text: async () => \"\" };\n476\t            if (url === \"/api/v1/surfaces/register\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ surface_id: mockSurfaceId }) };\n477\t            if (url.startsWith(\"/api/v1/sessions/\") && url.endsWith(\"/topics\")) return { ok: true, json: async () => ({ cards: topicsCards }) };\n478\t            if (url === \"/dispatch\") return { ok: true, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n479\t            return { ok: false, json: async () => ({}), text: async () => \"\" };\n480\t        };\n481\t    }\n482\t\n483\t    const htmlPath = path.resolve(__dirname, \"..\", \"..\", \"src\", \"canvas\", \"index.html\");\n484\t    const inline = extractInlineScript(fs.readFileSync(htmlPath, \"utf8\"));\n485\t    // runInThisContext so the inline script's free `document`/`window`/`fetch`/\n486\t    // `EventSource` resolve to the globals installed above — exactly the globals\n487\t    // a browser would provide.
